item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article has always frustrated me. So much. Not because it's badly written, not because of any grammar errors or stuff like that. It's a great article. But that equation… I don't know why, but the fact that there's nothing I can find that could calculate the answer to that equation… It upsets me so much.

Great article, though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the images appear to be modified pictures from here and here, which are both public domain and thus compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was one of the first few scips I saw and really, really liked when first joining - but coming back to it now, I feel like it's just lost something. It seemed (at least imho) to be far, far better when the reasons for the Extreme MathsTM were unspecified - maybe it would get the growth to stop, maybe it's just a 2001-esque intelligence/development test.

But having the maths problem definitely stop the thing sucks a lot of the mystery out of it, and just adds more questions (like, well, why would a lot bajillion prime factors be a kill switch for some von neumanns? what would you even need them for?). Plus, the outline of "why we think the maths'll stop it growing" at the bottom doesn't really seem necessary - although I suppose this is kind of ironic, considering how much of a fan I am of scientific/engineering techtalk in the likes of 1718.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solving X would be easy if P = NP so really there's two cool reasons to help find out if P = NP or not: A million bucks, and the prevention of the complete consumption of our lunar watcher and inevitable same fate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the cross testing really adds to the article here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm. So the best the Foundation can do is wait until computer technology catches up, and normalcy might be totally shot by then? Makes sense. Poor Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, thats the gist of it. But solving for 'x' (factoring an intractably large number into its prime components) is impossible short of using the anomalous computer mentioned which has its own risks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… I don't think I quite understand. It's aliens building a thing, but we can make them stop if we solve for x? (I understand the math is a little more complicated, but still.)

Reluctantly upvoting, despite math.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it is. Edit made

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A supernova is 10^44 joules IIRC

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Argh. Of course - it's been too long since I have mathed.

I'm'a just go dig my dunce cap out of the basement now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, its not in factorized form.
As an example:

2^2 + 3^5 = 247

the factored form of 247 is
13 x 19

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A'ight then… Can I ask something?

Isn't the original format the big f'in number is in already a prime decomposition? As in, it's pre-factorized?

If I understand this correctly, the machines are saying "transmit the prime factorization back to us asap or we eat yo' moon…", but they already transmitted it in a factorized manner?

What the hell did I miss here?

item.title: Fixed typo
item.content:encoded:
There was a typo in the skip:

"SCP-155 being a computer cable of of an asymptotic number of computations in finite amounts of time").

I think by cable he meant capable, so I fixed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The occasional math geek that I am has to upvote this for the mathematical formula alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
UPDATE: I've made some additions to this, using an idea suggested by draxal about cross-testing with SCP-155 Infinite Speed Computer. Hopefully it will be well recieved.

Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thank you thats really nice of you.
I've written three only. two scientific, one sort of magical realism
i have an author page

requite-fahrenheit-file

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow…

Your grasp of technical language and your ability to make scientifically plausible anomalies is really wonderful. Why haven't you written more?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. I really didnt think anyone would try to factor it. (As if I had hidden a real message in it somehow)
Im mean 2 to the ( 2 to the power of 89 ) ) is so HUGE….

But I commend you for at least analyzing the difficulty.

I think it would require an advance of Gaussian proportions to address this problem (if I may quote from the movie 'Sneakers')

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly thought that I was going to end up getting at least 30 different factors before I started having to plow through big numbers. The end result here was insanity. I was uncertain of what the problem with doing it was at first, now it's all too clear. The universe would literally end before my computer would be able to factor that.

In fact, working this out….you could cover the entire surface of every planet in the solar system, PLUS the sun, in 1000 computers per square meter, each of them 1000 times faster than my own, then take the age of the universe from start to finish(big bang to big crunch) in seconds, CUBE that number, and have every one of them run different parts of the problem for that long, and it STILL wouldn't be solved unless they started finding some factors in the 60 digit number range or lower…..

If that helps give you an idea of the difficulties….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but I monitor this thread closely! =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A necro/necropost is a comment that does something along the lines of a) replying to someone specific in a thread when that particular conversation ended awhile ago, or b) posting a comment that doesn't contribute to a discussion in a thread of an article that has been up for awhile with no currwntly ongoing discussion.

Or something along those lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: whats a necro?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This might be a necro, but I'm going to try my hand at it using the starter number you provided. Sorry if it's a necro.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I commend you for trying to factor it =D
If you end up succeeding, let me know before you transmit the factors….I'll need to prepare myself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Snerk*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

CHRIST ON A PROVERBIAL FLIPPING BIKE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the number:

+ show block
– hide block

88,924,158,227,561,274,535,616,027,826,704,189,741,797,194,245,632,693,189,246,748,802,243,081,077,828,444,672,080,641,956,067,453,907,607,985,465,284,677,054,289,058,542,757,047,443,419,787,490,427,514,267,881,413,760

the factors:

2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 3, 5, "oh god those are big numbers"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you've changed my mind. Editing now.
Thanks/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They teach you what "expression" means in 8th grade I think, at least where I'm from. And even if we're going for the lowest common denominator (in terms of people, not math), the Foundation would still use the term "expression".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: drat, I know you're right. But I think a lay audience would better recognize equation than expression….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To get pedantic about it, that's a mathematical expression, not an equation. There's no assertion of equality between any expressions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the same thing. Try allowing scripts from mathjax.org on your script blocker, then refresh the cache and cookies for the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i can't agree with you I'm afraid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The only thing I don't like about it is its obvious "space alien" nature. It sort of drags all the other great, mysterious and even frightening aspects down. IMO, the removal of the reference to an impact being witnessed, as well as the probe speculation, would crank everything up a notch or two.

item.title: Re: I read the book
item.content:encoded: Are you trying to accuse the author of plagiarism or are you just noting interesting incidental similiarities?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: test

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, I like the idea. But as Scantron points out, story would go *poof*
I would like to know what the factors are, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know, I know, no cross testing.

Reasonable crosstesting (which is what you're proposing) is fine. The only problem is that this would solve the problem (the story problem, not the math problem). It's a cool idea that I think would work, I just don't think it'd be something we'd put in either article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know, I know, no cross testing.

But, hear me out:

Put SCP-155 either in space, or in an environment where its release of energy after running
wouldn't effect anything, power it up, use it to figure out the equation, and sha-bam, we're all good.

item.title: I read the book
item.content:encoded: This article has marked similarities to Roger Macbride Allen's The Ring Of Charon (minus the gravity wave lasers).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
strange….the code for it is simply:

 > [[size larger ]] [[$ 2^{2^{79}} + 3^{2^{83}} + 5^{2^{89}} + 7^{2^{97}} $]] [[/size]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but something about this page (probably the equation at the bottom) always makes my computer freeze up when it's loading. Any idea why?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. I think I know who Sylandro is (they're the solar company which caused a scandal for the Obama administration, correct?)
But I don't see the connection to the SCP…? Elucidation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, I liked this. =)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damnit did the Sylandro screw up another probe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: test

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Know what.. .I really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, given that it already covers and area of 250,000 square km (roughly a diameter of 5000 km), honestly, what can we hope to do to it….

Wikipedia tells me 250,000 square km is roughly the area of the United Kingdom… =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is highly advanced alien technology that survived the perils of space travel and went from zero to fission reactors in a single decade using nothing but moon rock and vacumn. Who's to say that a nuke would hurt it? Heck, the residual radiation and heavy elements from a nuke might help them expand faster, they might consider it a gift rather than an attack.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that you're right…I was reluctant to add it, though, because its just going to resemble all the traditional boilerplate that you see in other SCP's, like:
Foundation personnel at NASA and the European Space Agency are tasked with…blah,blah,blah….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An example of crosslinking done well is the misters. Each mister has a link to SCP-909 which links to every other little mister on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't that the sub-plot of Transformers 3? And Apollo 19 (or whatever that other awful "MONSTERS ON THE MOON" movie recently was?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for Von Neuman probe, and math that made my head hurt at its size just looking at the equation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a pretty fuckin' terrifying concept, I like it a lot!
Of course, if I were on the O5 council, I'd have us nuke the whole fuckin' thing from orbit.
Just to be sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, with the right sidelong references, one could add a nice Easter egg here. Imply, very carefully, that it's the reason Apollo was never repeated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter is a real thing that belongs to NASA, the Containment Procedures needs something about a NASA liaison keeping the SCP secret.

Plus maybe the Containment Procedures should have something about preventing/interfering with anyone who wants to land a probe on the far side of the moon or put anything else in orbit around the moon.
